data.title: UK CfD AR7 awarded a record 8.4 GW of offshore wind capacity across 8 projects, signaling new economic realities.
data.description: In Denmark, the UK government announced the outcomes of Contracts for Difference Allocation Round 7 (AR7), showing a continuous interest in UK offshore wind and the challenges of submitting cost-efficient, viable bids. The allocation round assigned a historic 8.4 gigawatts of offshore wind capacity among eight projects, following a budget boost by the government. The majority of the capacity (8.2 GW) was granted to the aforementioned projects.

data.title: INJ price predicted to reach $6.20 in February with short-term target at $5.90 and critical support at $5.02. Bullish setup.
data.description: The predicted prices for INJ are as follows: a short-term target of $5.90 within the next week, a medium-term forecast ranging between $6.00 and $6.20 over the next month. The bullish breakout level is set at $5.90, while critical support is noted at $5.02.

data.title: Calavo Growers surges 13% in after-hours trading to $25.50 on merger news with Mission Produce acquiring them for $27 per share.
data.description: Calavo Growers Inc. (NASDAQ: CVGW) experienced a significant increase of 12.93% in after-hours trading, reaching $25.50, subsequent to the reveal of its latest financial outcomes and the announcement of a merger pact with Mission Produce Inc. (NASDAQ: AVO) headquartered in California. The agreed terms of the merger state that Mission Produce will purchase Calavo Growers at $27 per share, established on Mission's 30-trading-day volume-weighted average price concluding on Jan. 13. As part of the deal, Calavo shareholders will receive $14.85 in cash as well as 0.9790 shares of Mission for each Calavo share they hold. Post the finalization of the agreement, Mission Produce stakeholders will possess around 80.3% of the merged entity, with Calavo Growers shareholders retaining about 19.7%. The international food corporation foresees...

data.title: BitsLab audited over 200 projects in 2025, discovering 2,858 vulnerabilities, ensuring Web3 infrastructure security.
data.description: In San Francisco on January 15, 2026, it was noted that the year 2025 witnessed significant advancements in Web3 infrastructure. One of the key players in this evolution, BitsLab, has set a precedent in AI-driven digital asset security. The company has been instrumental in ensuring the safety of Web3 infrastructure by conducting audits, penetration testing, and ongoing security surveillance. As of now, the exact number of audits conducted or the extent of security monitoring efforts were not specified.

data.title: Goldman Sachs to report Q4 earnings of $11.67/share, revenue estimate $14.12 billion ($13.87B last year). Shares down 0.6%.
data.description: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. (NYSE: GS) is set to announce its fourth-quarter earnings on Thursday, Jan. 15. Analysts are predicting earnings of $11.67 per share, a decrease from $11.95 per share in the previous year. The consensus estimate for quarterly revenue is $14.12 billion, up from $13.87 billion last year. The company has surpassed analyst estimates for earnings per share in the last nine quarters and for revenue in the past 10 quarters. Despite this, Goldman Sachs' shares closed at $932.67 on Wednesday, down by 0.6%. For more details and analyst ratings, readers can refer to the Analyst Stock Ratings page.

data.title: BTC ETFs see unprecedented $1.7B inflow amidst BTC surge over $97K, hitting 2-month high and boosting bullish sentiment.
data.description: In a remarkable three-day stretch, spot Bitcoin exchange-traded funds (ETFs) amassed more than $1.7 billion in investments, coinciding with Bitcoin reaching its highest value in two months. This surge in capital influx occurred as market sentiment shifted towards optimism and bullishness regarding the future of the cryptocurrency.
